name: B-52H Stratofortress
detail: USAF heavy bomber deployed from Diego Garcia and Whiteman AFB to conduct strategic bombing operations in Afghanistan since October 2001. Used for carpet bombing of al-Qaeda positions at Tora Bora and providing close air support throughout the war. Capable of carrying up to 70,000 lbs of munitions.

name: AC-130 Spectre/Spooky Gunship
detail: US Air Force fixed-wing gunship used extensively for close air support of ground forces in Afghanistan. Armed with 25mm cannon, 40mm Bofors, and 105mm howitzer. Controversially used in October 2015 Kunduz MSF hospital bombing that killed 42 people. Provides persistent fire support for SOF operations.

name: MQ-9 Reaper RPAS
detail: Primary US combat drone used for both ISR and strike missions in Afghanistan and Pakistan. Carries Hellfire missiles and GBU-12 laser-guided bombs. CIA used Reapers for most drone strikes in FATA. US went from 40 drones in 2001 to over 7,000 by 2013. Killed numerous al-Qaeda and TTP leadership figures including Baitullah Mehsud.

name: CH-47 Chinook Helicopter
detail: Heavy-lift helicopter essential for moving troops and supplies in Afghanistan's mountainous terrain where roads were absent or too dangerous. Used by US, British, Canadian, and other NATO forces. A Chinook was shot down August 6, 2011, killing 30 Americans including 22 Navy SEALs — the single deadliest incident of the war for US forces.

name: M1A2 Abrams / Challenger 2 Tank
detail: Coalition main battle tanks were deployed in limited numbers to Afghanistan despite terrain challenges. Canadian Leopard 2s were particularly prominent in Helmand operations. British Challenger 2s served in Helmand. Tanks proved useful in clearing Taliban-held urban areas but were logistically challenging to support.

name: MRAP (Mine-Resistant Ambush Protected)
detail: The US deployed over 6,000 MRAP vehicles to Afghanistan as V-shaped hulls provided protection against IEDs which were Taliban's most lethal weapon. Development was rushed in response to spiraling IED casualties. Cost $1 million+ each. Most were destroyed or abandoned during withdrawal; Taliban inherited a fraction.

name: A-10 Thunderbolt II (Warthog)
detail: USAF close air support aircraft extensively deployed to Afghanistan for ground attack missions. Known for its 30mm GAU-8 Avenger rotary cannon. A-10 was involved in the September 2006 Panjwai friendly fire incident killing one Canadian soldier during Operation Medusa. Highly valued by ground troops for precision close air support.

name: PAF F-16 Fighting Falcon
detail: Pakistan Air Force's primary combat aircraft used for counterterrorism strikes in FATA, KPK, and Afghan border areas. US provided Pakistan F-16s as part of military aid conditional on counterterrorism cooperation. Used in March 2024 strikes in Paktika province, Afghanistan, targeting TTP camps — crossing an escalatory threshold.

name: GBU-43/B MOAB (Mother of All Bombs)
detail: Massive Ordnance Air Blast bomb weighing 21,600 lbs — largest non-nuclear conventional bomb ever used in combat. Dropped on ISIS-K tunnel complex in Achin district, Nangarhar on April 13, 2017. Killed approximately 94 ISIS-K militants according to Afghan government. First combat use of the weapon; signaled loosened targeting rules under Trump.

name: Hellfire R9X 'Flying Ginsu'
detail: CIA modified Hellfire missile with pop-out blades instead of explosive warhead, designed for precision killing of single targets with minimal collateral damage. Used to kill al-Qaeda leader Ayman al-Zawahiri on his Kabul balcony on July 31, 2022, destroying only the balcony while leaving the building largely intact. Represents US over-the-horizon precision capability.

name: Provincial Reconstruction Teams (PRTs)
detail: Approximately 26 NATO PRTs operated across Afghanistan combining military and civilian personnel for development and governance support. Led by different nations (UK in Helmand, Germany in Kunduz, US in various). SIGAR found PRTs created dependency rather than Afghan capacity, and most projects were unsustainable. PRTs dismantled during 2013-2014 transition.

name: MV-22 Osprey Tiltrotor
detail: US Marine Corps tiltrotor aircraft combining helicopter vertical takeoff with fixed-wing speed used in Afghanistan for assault missions and troop insertion. Faster than helicopters for medical evacuation and personnel transport. First combat deployment of Osprey was in Afghanistan. Faced mechanical reliability criticisms but performed well in theater.

name: M777 Lightweight Howitzer
detail: 155mm lightweight howitzer used extensively by US and Afghan forces for fire support. Helicopter-portable due to titanium construction; crucial for fire support in remote mountain areas. Later transferred to Ukraine. Afghan forces' M777s were abandoned during 2021 collapse; Taliban displayed several captured examples.

name: Taliban Motorcycle Courier & IED Network
detail: Taliban's primary military-logistics asset: motorcycle-borne fighters using simple IEDs, RPGs, and small arms. IEDs accounted for 60%+ of coalition casualties. The motorcycle network enabled rapid movement of fighters and materials. Impossible to interdict without targeting civilians. Taliban's asymmetric approach negated much of coalition's technological advantage.

name: JSOC Task Force (TF 714 / TF 373)
detail: Joint Special Operations Command task forces conducted night raids, direct action, and high-value target operations throughout Afghanistan. At peak (2010-2012) conducted 10-12 raids per night. Responsible for capturing/killing thousands of Taliban and al-Qaeda leaders. DEVGRU (SEAL Team 6) conducted bin Laden raid; Delta Force led many Haqqani Network targeting operations.
